concerns.text: FR-008 (Fallback Mechanism) requires calculating a 'Global Exposure' metric using 'aggregate population data' if birth years are missing. No task exists to implement this calculation or fetch the required data source. T023 only sets a flag and logs a warning, explicitly stating 'no data source is defined'. The spec requirement is unimplemented.

Tasks T057 (Effect Size) and T058 (Assumption Validation) are listed as active unchecked tasks in Phase 6. However, the spec (FR-005, SC-003) does not mandate Cohen's d or specific assumption tests (Shapiro-Wilk/Breusch-Pagan) as deliverables. These tasks represent scope creep and lack a corresponding FR/SC anchor, while the actual FR-008 requirement remains unmet.

Task T036 implements a logic branch that treats the config value '[deferred]' as a string and logs a 'check was deferred' warning. While this handles the placeholder, the task description implies a bypass of the numeric comparison logic required by SC-004 when a value IS present. The task should explicitly state it performs the numeric comparison when the config is not '[deferred]' to ensure full SC-004 coverage.

Task T044 (Sensitivity Analysis) states it must 'Load Data' from pre-computed `ingested_cohort.parquet`. FR-006 requires re-running the 'entire matching and aggregation process'. If the frequency filter (T015) or matching thresholds alter the set of tracks, the pre-computed `residualized_exposure_score` (which depends on the track list) may be invalid. The task description needs to clarify if it re-calculates exposure scores or if the spec/plan needs to define a stable track set.
